titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Herman Kahn Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Matthew T. McLean

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday February 12, 2020 at 08:26

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, writings, subject files, and photographs which document the professional life of Herman Kahn, especially during the years after he left the Franklin Delano Roosevelt Presidential Library. The files mostly pertain to Kahn's involvement with professional organizations and his interest in issues affecting the archival profession as a whole.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Herman Kahn's office files at the time of his death, 1975.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: While the collection as a whole is available for research, parts of it may be restricted. Any restricted material will be noted as such.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Herman Kahn Papers. Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in six series: I. Correspondence, 1963-1975. II. Professional Organizations, 1962-1975. III. United States Government, 1950-1975. IV. Writings, 1953-1975. V. Yale University, 1968-1975. VI. Personal and Photographs, 1950-1976.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Herman Kahn was born in Rochester, Minnesota in 1907. He received his B.A. and M.A. from the University of Minnesota in in 1928 and 1930, respectively. From 1931 to 1933 he was an assistant professor of history at Nebraska State Teachers College. Kahn then served as associate historian for the National Park Service until 1936. In 1936 he entered the archival profession and worked at the National Archives, serving as chief of the Division of Interior Department Archives from 1942 to 1947. From 1948 to 1961 Kahn was the director of the Franklin D. Roosevelt Library in Hyde Park, New York, after which he served as a special assistant to the National Archivist and Chief of Civil Archives. From 1964 to 1968 he was assistant archivist for Presidential Libraries for the National Archives Service. After a brief retirement in 1968, Kahn became associate librarian for Manuscripts and Archives in the Yale University Library, where he served until his death in 1975. Kahn was a forceful advocate of the idea that the archival profession did not merely serve the needs of historians, but was a profession in its own right. Occasionally he was called to testify before the United States Congress on matters relating to the profession. Kahn's specialties were presidential libraries and the history of the New Deal, and he wrote several articles on these and other topics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of correspondence, writings, subject files, and photographs which document the professional life of Herman Kahn, especially during the years after he left the Franklin Delano Roosevelt Presidential Library. The files mostly pertain to Kahn's involvement with professional organizations and his interest in issues affecting the archival profession as a whole.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series is arranged in alphabetical order by last name of correspondent.

did.unittitle: Berner, Richard C. [re: attempt to found a new archivists' organization]
Collier, Christopher [re: Association for the Study of Connecticut History]
Rhoads, James B. [Archivist of the United States]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series is arranged in alphabetical order by organization, and contains materials of various professional committees, organizations, and conferences with which Kahn worked. Materials relating to conferences and to specific archives are grouped together in alphabetical order under those headings.

did.unittitle: Association of Research Libraries Committee on Access to Manuscripts and Rare Books
Conference on National Archives and Statistical Research
International Council on Archives Extraordinary Congress
National Archives Conference on Captured German and Related Records
New World Balance and the Search for Peace in the Middle East
Joint Organization of American Historians-American Historical Association-Society of American Archivists Committee on Historians and Archives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains materials pertaining to Kahn's involvement with the United States government, and is arranged in alphabetical order by subject.

did.unittitle: Correspondence [re: pending legislation; includes correspondence with Birch Bayh and Bella Abzug]
Government Archives [publications relating to contents of]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains various published articles and written speeches by Kahn. The first folder contains several bibliographies of his writings.

did.unittitle: Yale Alumni Magazine [re: Manuscripts and Archives]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains material related to Kahn's time at Yale University, and is arranged in alphabetical order by subject.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Series V is restricted until January 1, 2025, as established by Yale Corporation regulations.

did.unittitle: Report of the Library Committee of the Department of History

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains material related to Kahn's personal life. There is one folder of photographs at the end of the series.
